content: The orthographic and linguistic ambiguity of the term 'reflexion' (as both an older English spelling variant of reflection and a standalone autonomous AI agent framework) can be leveraged to design a self-correcting LLM self-reflection mechanism that roots out persistent reasoning errors by confronting the agent with its own inconsistent outputs from alternate 'spelling variants' of its reflection step. This dual framing turns a minor linguistic oddity into a structured multi-perspective reflection prompt that requires the LLM to reconcile conflicting outputs from its own reflection routines.
